summary: The initiative delivers a single‑HTML‑file, browser‑based Sudoku game that lets users select a difficulty, fill the grid, track time and mistakes, and view a completion screen. It is intended for internal demo audiences and must work offline on both desktop and mobile browsers.

assumptions.text: We assume puzzles are hard‑coded in the HTML file because no generator is required.
We assume only vanilla JavaScript/CSS can be used; no external libraries are permitted.
We assume the game must operate fully offline with no network requests.
We assume the timer and mistake counter are client‑side only and do not need to be persisted across sessions.

requirementsInterpreted.text: Render a 9x9 Sudoku grid with clear 3x3 sub‑box borders.
Allow the user to start a new puzzle by selecting a difficulty level (easy, medium, hard).
Prevent editing of fixed starting numbers while permitting entry of numbers 1‑9 in other cells via keyboard or on‑screen controls.
Highlight the selected cell’s row, column, and sub‑box, and optionally flag duplicate numbers or conflicts.
Provide a timer that starts on the first move, can be paused/resumed, and a mistake counter that increments on invalid moves.
Detect puzzle completion and display a completion screen showing elapsed time, difficulty, and total mistakes.
Include reset and new‑puzzle buttons that preserve the chosen difficulty level.

outOfScope.text: Adding multiplayer, leaderboards, or social features.
Supporting user‑created or imported custom puzzles.
Integrating external analytics or tracking services.
Using external JavaScript libraries or frameworks.

riskFlags.text: Missing detailed goals and acceptance criteria may lead to scope creep.
The single‑file constraint limits future extensibility and addition of external resources.
Prohibiting external libraries increases development effort for all UI and logic components.
Offline‑only requirement could cause usability issues on devices where JavaScript is disabled.
